rationale: Schema-doc parity (R4): interfaces.md § Panel Artifact Schema matches internal/panel constants exactly — panel.FileName="panel.json"; verdictFileRE=`^(.+)-round-([1-9][0-9]*)\.json$` (1-based, R1-round-0.json rejected, doc states the same); ConsolidatedName=consolidated-round-N.md. No contradicting pattern. Parser-parity (R5/R6): spec.go's checkImpactedDomainsResolutionParity is byte-identical to plan.go — same loadImpactedDomains (contextpack.ParseSpec), same normalizeImpactedDomains(nil,root,"",impacted), same errors.Unwrap/os.IsNotExist load handling, same impacted-domains-resolve code; touchpoint check reuses newMemoStore(adrStoreForSpecFn) like the plan citation gate, existence-only, anchored-link-only regex. Spec-approve consumes no normalized set downstream and emits no adr-coverage-*/adr-cite-irrelevant (R7c) — leniency-preserving, never stricter. Empirically (binary + /tmp scratch w/ real domains+adr): path-like unclaimed domain -> impacted-domains-resolve ERROR (blocks); bare-name-no-manifest -> TOLERATED (R5 survives); anchored [ADR-9999](..) -> adr-touchpoint-missing ERROR; bare-prose ADR-9999 -> TOLERATED (R6 survives); real 110 spec (ADR-0040 prose + workflow domain) passes clean. Types: tallyExitAction switches on value-type Decision.Action (no nil-deref); revParseForPanelFn seam clean.
findings: R4 schema parity: interfaces.md:69/87/96 vs panel.go:34, tally.go:33/38 — panel.json, `^(.+)-round-([1-9][0-9]*)\.json$`, consolidated-round-N.md all agree; doc explicitly names round-0 as nonconforming/rejected, matching the [1-9] leading-digit anchor.

R5 parity: spec.go:216-228 checkImpactedDomainsResolutionParity mirrors plan.go:125-146 call-for-call (loadImpactedDomains->contextpack.ParseSpec; normalizeImpactedDomains(nil,root,"",impacted); impacted-domains-resolve / impacted-domains-load with identical errors.Unwrap+os.IsNotExist guard).

R5 empirical: /tmp/g2-scratch spec 201 bare-name 'totallymadeupdomain' -> no resolve error (only benign lifecycle-binding warn) = TOLERATED; spec 200 path-like 'internal/zzz_nonexistent/foo.go' -> impacted-domains-resolve severity-0 = BLOCKS spec-approve.

R6 parity: adrTouchpointLinkRe requires `[...](...)` anchor; checkADRTouchpointsExist uses newMemoStore(adrStoreForSpecFn(root,specDir)) (spec.go:270) — same store the plan citation gate builds (plan.go:156); distinct code adr-touchpoint-missing, existence-only, no adr-coverage-*.

R6 empirical: spec 202 anchored [ADR-9999](../../adr/ADR-9999.md) -> adr-touchpoint-missing ERROR; spec 203 bare-prose 'ADR-9999' (no anchor) -> no error = TOLERATED; real 110 spec's ADR-0040 prose mention passes `validate spec` clean.

R7c leniency: spec-approve does not consume the normalized domain set and emits neither adr-coverage-missing nor adr-cite-irrelevant — behavior-identical-merely-earlier, never a stricter rule than plan-approve (case 201 produced no coverage error at spec-approve).

Types: tallyExitAction (panel.go:526) and renderPanelTally switch over value-type panel.Decision.Action (Allow/default->nil, Warn->advisory+nil, Block->guard.NewFailure); revParseForPanelFn (panel.go:37) is a clean package-var seam; no nil-deref in the new cmd paths.
